---
title: "Karl-Emil Bendtsen"
about:
  template: trestles        # or jolla, solana, etc.
  image: pb.jpg        # put this file in the project folder
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "kesb@ifs.ku.dk"
    # - icon: github
    #   text: GitHub
    #   href: "https://github.com/your-username"
    - icon: google scholar
      text: Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=jsZh-bsAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao"
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: "https://x.com/KarlEmil8D"
    - icon: bluesky
      text: Bluesky
      href: "https://bsky.app/profile/karlemilbendtsen.bsky.social" 
---

Hi! I am a PhD student at the Department of Political Science at the University of Copenhagen. My research is mainly on bureaucracy, discretion, policy uptake, legal complexity, digitalization and the use of AI in public administrations and politics. I sometimes also do reserach on electoral systems and local politics. 

## Publications

- [*Women’s causal impact through the policy process: Different preferences – same outcomes*](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0261379426000648) with Frederik Klaaborg Kjøller & Lene Holm Pedersen. (Electoral Studies)

- [*Increasing take-up of social benefits: a meta-analysis of field experiments*](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1002/pam.70085) (Journal of Policy Analysis and Managment) — [updated results](takeup_update.html)

- [*Public Administration Beyond Public Administration Journals*](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/ps-political-science-and-politics/article/public-administration-beyond-public-administration-journals/9A9373268F2880A786AB899960764015) with Asmus Leth Olsen & Paul van Leeuwen. (PS: Political Science and Politics) — [additional analyses](https://paopa.org)


## Working papers

- *Longer Laws, Better Service? Legislative Complexity and Bureaucratic Quality* with Jonas Krogh Madsen

- *Arbitrary Bureaucratic Decisions*

- *Noise in Administrative Decision-Making: Public Administration’s Unseen Error and AI’s Role in Enhancing Personnel Selection Consistency* with Mogens Jin Pedersen & Florian Keppeler

- *Can Nominating More Minority Candidates Backfire?* with Jacob Richard Bøggild Strabo
